model_essays.title: 张天明上大学的第一天
model_essays.text: 张天明是大学一年级的新生，他家离大学很远。大学快开学了，他坐飞机去学校。他坐飞机坐了两个多小时，下飞机以后，马上叫了一辆出租汽车，很快就到了学校宿舍。宿舍前边的人很多，在那儿，他认识了柯林。
model_essays.summary: A short narrative recounting Zhang Tianming's first day at college: a far-from-home freshman who flies in, takes a taxi, and meets Ke Lin at the busy dorm.
model_essays.why_study: A compact model of how to make a multi-sentence narrative cohere: it shows pronoun substitution (他 for 张天明), omission of repeated subjects when they stay the same, and the use of time and place words to chain events.
key_devices: Pronoun 他 replaces the named subject once it is established
Repeated identical subject pronouns are dropped (the textbook bracketed [他] before 下飞机/马上/很快)
Time/sequence connectors: …以后, 马上, 快…了, 就
Place word 在那儿 as a connective hinge that introduces the meeting with Ke Lin
Verb-duration complement 坐了两个多小时 to express elapsed time

model_essays.title: 张天明的宿舍很方便
model_essays.text: 张天明的宿舍很小，因为没有空调，很热，但是很方便。学生餐厅就在楼下，旁边有一个小商店，卖日用品和文具。这里洗衣服也方便得很，每层楼有三台洗衣机和三台干衣机。离教室也不远，走路差不多五、六分钟。这里离大马路很远，很安静。附近还有一家中国餐馆儿，菜很地道。
model_essays.summary: A paragraph arguing that despite being small, hot, and air-conditioner-less, the dorm is very convenient, listing the cafeteria, shop, laundry, classrooms, quiet, and a nearby authentic restaurant.
model_essays.why_study: A clean example of stating a thesis (很方便) and then supporting it with concrete, parallel evidence. It models how place words, conjunctions, and adverbs knit a description into a flowing whole.
key_devices: Concession then claim: 很小…很热，但是很方便
Place words as connectives: 楼下, 旁边, 这里, 离…
Conjunctions 因为/但是 to join clauses; adverbs 就/还/也 as cohesion markers
Degree complement 方便得很 for emphasis
Approximation 差不多五、六分钟 to make detail vivid without false precision

model_essays.title: 宿舍房间描写（张天明的房间）
model_essays.text: 张天明宿舍的房间不太大，住两个人。他的同屋已经来了。房间里家具不多，靠窗户摆着两张书桌，每张桌子的前边有一把椅子。书桌的旁边是床，床上有被子和毯子。床旁边有两个衣柜，柜子里挂着一些衣服。门右边放着两个书架，书架还是空的。
model_essays.summary: A systematic description of a two-person dorm room, moving from desks by the window to the bed, wardrobes, and bookshelves by the door.
model_essays.why_study: The canonical model for describing a space using existential sentences. It shows how to lay out a room object by object in a logical spatial path so a reader could draw it.
key_devices: Existential pattern: Place + verb + 着/了 + numeral + measure word + noun (靠窗户摆着两张书桌 / 柜子里挂着一些衣服 / 门右边放着两个书架)
Spatial anchors chained one to the next: 靠窗户→旁边→床上→床旁边→门右边
Measure-word precision: 张 (desk), 把 (chair), 个 (wardrobe/bookshelf)
是 vs 有 to signal a single item vs. multiple items at a location

model_essays.title: 在饭馆儿（点菜叙事开头）
model_essays.text: 今天是周末，功课也做完了，张天明就给柯林发微信，说他和他的女朋友丽莎想吃中国饭，问他想不想去。柯林说正好他也想吃中国饭，就让张天明和丽莎在宿舍门口等着，他开车来接他们。半个钟头以后，柯林的汽车到了。张天明和丽莎上车一看，车里还有一个女孩儿，柯林介绍说，她是从中国来的留学生，叫林雪梅。几分钟以后，他们四个人到了那家中国饭馆儿。
model_essays.summary: A narrative setup: on a free weekend Zhang Tianming WeChats Ke Lin about getting Chinese food; Ke Lin drives over, and they discover a fourth person, exchange student Lin Xuemei, before arriving at the restaurant.
model_essays.why_study: A model for the narrative 'frame' that sets up a scene before dialogue. It shows topic-fronting of known information and tight action sequencing with time phrases.
key_devices: Topic-comment opening with given information fronted: 今天是周末，功课也做完了
就 to mark the natural next action (发微信 / 让…等着)
Momentary 一 + verb: 上车一看 ('took one look and saw…')
Time-stamped sequencing: 半个钟头以后…几分钟以后…
Reported speech with 说/问/介绍说 to compress dialogue into narration

model_essays.title: 住校外的三个理由（柯林的回答）
model_essays.text: 有的人喜欢住在学校宿舍，觉得又方便又安全，有的人喜欢住在校外，因为校外的房子比较便宜。我住在校外，除了想省点儿钱以外，还因为住在校外自由。再说，住在校内也不见得很方便。
model_essays.summary: Ke Lin explains why he lives off campus: some prefer dorms for convenience and safety, others off campus for cheaper rent; he himself lives off campus to save money and for freedom, adding that on-campus living isn't necessarily convenient.
model_essays.why_study: A textbook demonstration of stacking connectives to deliver an organized, persuasive answer rather than a flat list. The same content is also shown reorganized with 第一/第二/第三, illustrating two equivalent structuring strategies.
key_devices: Contrast framing: 有的人…，有的人…
Reason chain: 因为 + 除了…以外，还 + 再说
Polite hedge 不见得 to soften a counter-claim
Paired-adjective 又方便又安全
Alternative enumeration via 第一…，第二…，第三… (shown as a variant)

grammar_patterns.explanation: Chains multiple reasons for fluent extended speech: 因为 introduces the cause, 除了…以外，还 adds a further reason, and 再说 tacks on a final supporting point. The whole set can be replaced by ordinal enumeration 第一…，第二…，第三….
examples: 我住在校外，除了想省点儿钱以外，还因为住在校外自由。再说，住在校内也不见得很方便。
妈妈告诉我当医生最好。因为除了好找工作以外，还挣钱多，再说家里人看病也方便。

grammar_patterns.explanation: An adverb placed before the predicate to politely express a dissenting or qualifying opinion — 'not necessarily so.'
examples: 在中国出生的人，中文不见得好。
报上说的不见得对。
开车不见得比走快。

grammar_patterns.pattern: X对Y（没）有好处 (X is (not) good for Y)
grammar_patterns.explanation: Fixed frame for stating benefit or harm; 好处 is a noun, 对 introduces the beneficiary.
examples: 少开车，多走路，对健康有好处。
这样做对你没有好处。
上过大学、会用电脑、会说外语，对找工作有好处。

grammar_patterns.pattern: Existential sentence: Place + verb + 着/了 + numeral + MW + noun
grammar_patterns.explanation: Describes what exists at a location, with the place fronted and an indefinite noun at the end. Verbs are limited to 有, 是, and posture/placement verbs (放, 摆, 挂, 站, 坐, 躺, 拿, 贴). 是 implies a single/sole item; 有 implies one of possibly several.
examples: 桌子上放着一本书。
书架上摆着三张照片。
门右边放着两个书架。
他身上穿着一件白衬衫，手里拿着一条红毯子。

grammar_patterns.explanation: Expresses a general, non-specific degree ('rather, fairly'). It must NOT be used for an explicit comparison against a stated standard (where 更 or 一点儿 is used instead).
examples: 这把椅子比较贵，你别买了。
我比较喜欢打网球。
周末学校宿舍一般都有一点吵，图书馆比较安静。

grammar_patterns.explanation: A degree complement after adjectives or cognitive/emotive verbs indicating an extreme extent; stronger than 很 + adjective.
examples: 大家都忙得很。
我们的宿舍安静得很。
张天明想家得很。
学生餐厅的饭难吃得很。

grammar_patterns.pattern: 一 + verb  /  一…就… (as soon as)
grammar_patterns.explanation: 一 before a (usually monosyllabic) verb marks a brief, momentary action and needs a following clause. 一…就… links two consecutive actions; 就 is required when actions are immediate, subjects differ, or the first clause states a condition; optional when there is a pause with an adverbial.
examples: 我开门一看，是送信的。
老师刚才说的话，我一听就懂了。
我同屋每天一回宿舍就做功课。
他一到，我们就走。
我一吃味精就得喝很多水。

grammar_patterns.explanation: Joins two simultaneous qualities (adjectives, which must be same polarity) or two concurrent actions (verbs).
examples: 觉得又方便又安全。
妈妈做的清蒸鱼又嫩又香。
孩子们又跑又跳，玩儿得非常高兴。
那个小孩又哭又吵。

grammar_patterns.explanation: Stressed 是 placed before an adjective or verb concedes/reaffirms a previously stated point, typically before a 'but' clause. Requires that the predicate was already mentioned.
examples: 住在这个宿舍是很方便，去上课，去医院，去餐厅都不远。
他们的上海菜是很地道，可是太贵了。
他是搬走了，他去了一个更有名的大学。

grammar_patterns.explanation: Given/old information is placed at the front of the sentence as the topic and then commented on. Common for objects already in discourse, replacing plain SVO order.
examples: 今天是周末，功课也做完了……
这本书你看完了吗？
这件事我早就知道了。
你去台北的飞机票买好了吗？

grammar_patterns.explanation: A frame giving a flexible/indirect answer, deferring the outcome to some condition.
examples: 什么菜好吃？这就要看你的口味了。
明天的考试难不难？这就要看你准备得怎么样了。
这就要看买得到买不到便宜的机票了。

grammar_patterns.pattern: 是…的 (focusing on circumstances of a past event)
grammar_patterns.explanation: Used to ask about or highlight the time, place, manner, or companion of a known past action.
examples: 你是几号回学校来的？
我是二十三号回学校来的。
我是在美国出生，在美国长大的。

grammar_patterns.pattern: 适应 / 对…适应 (to get used to)
grammar_patterns.explanation: The verb 适应 must take a direct object, or the object may be fronted with 对 placed before the verb.
examples: 你适应大学的生活了吗？
你对这里的天气已经适应了吗？
我们刚来美国，对美国的天气还不适应。

expressions.register: colloquial, mildly dismissive — use only among familiars
neutral; softens bad news or a polite warning

expressions.meaning: may I trouble you to…; could you please

expressions.register: colloquial; note Taiwan/southern variant 道地

expressions.meaning: besides; what's more (adding a further reason)
to joke, to crack a joke

essay_takeaways: Don't string bare clauses together — make multi-sentence Chinese cohere with explicit connectives: 因为, 除了…以外，还, 再说, 但是, 所以. Spoken Chinese may drop them, but written prose should keep them.
Two interchangeable ways to organize reasons: the connective chain (因为…除了…以外，还…再说…) or ordinal enumeration (第一…，第二…，第三…). Pick one and stay consistent within a passage.

For cohesion across sentences, lean on three device families the textbook highlights: pronoun substitution (他 for the named subject), omission of a repeated identical subject, and time/place words (…以后, 马上, 快…了, 在那儿, 旁边, 这里) acting as connective hinges.

To describe a space, use existential sentences (Place + verb + 着/了 + numeral + MW + noun) and walk the reader along one logical spatial path (window → desk → bed → wardrobe → door) so they could redraw the room.
Front known/old information as the topic; introduce only new information in the comment. This 'given-before-new' ordering is what makes Chinese discourse flow naturally.
Structure an opinion as thesis-then-evidence: state the claim (e.g. 很方便) and back it with concrete, parallel details rather than vague assertions.
Soften disagreement and bad news with hedges — 不见得 (not necessarily), 恐怕 (I'm afraid), and reaffirmative 是…可是… (it is X, but…) — to sound polite rather than blunt.
Add color and rhythm with paired structures (又…又…) and degree complements (…得很), and keep numeric detail natural with 差不多 rather than false precision.
Compress dialogue into narration using reported speech verbs (说, 问, 介绍说) and sequence actions tightly with 一 + verb, 就, and time stamps (半个钟头以后…几分钟以后…).
